title: abies forrestii subsp. longibracteata
intro: Abies forrestii subsp. longibracteata, commonly known as Longbract Chinese Fir, is a subspecies of coniferous tree in the family Pinaceae, native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and other bioactive compounds. Longbract Chinese Fir has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herbal remedy for treating various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and needles are often used to make teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
